summary: Crisp soundboard captures an outstanding 1984 performance highlighted by a smoking Help‑Slip‑Frank sequence, soaring Morning Dew and stellar Brent on keys.
review: The recording is praised as one of the best‑sounding soundboards on the archive – clear, deep and virtually hiss‑free. Musically the show shines especially in the second set, with a tight Help > Slip > Frank transition, an inspired Space into Morning Dew, and strong contributions from Brent on keyboards. A few reviewers note Jerry’s voice sounding raspy on Eyes of the World, but overall the performance is regarded as a true 1984 gem.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews (many 5‑star) praise both the exceptional soundboard quality and the high‑energy, standout second set; minor vocal concerns do not detract from the overall excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, deep rich sound with very little hiss; reviewers call it one of the best soundboards heard on the site and note superb clarity throughout.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is highlighted as exceptional, featuring a tight Help‑Slip‑Frank sequence that flows into an expansive Space > Morning Dew jam; overall pacing is praised for its energy and cohesion.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice noted as slightly raspy on Eyes of the World but guitar work and overall vocal presence are praised; some say his voice hadn't yet failed at this point.
band_member_comments.Phil: Singing on “Do It in the Road” is highlighted as fun and indicative of the band’s good mood.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent's organ/keyboards receive strong praise, especially during Morning Dew and space jams, providing an awesome finish.
band_member_comments.Drums: Post‑drum sections described as tight, powerful and a highlight of the second set.
